reviews.review_text: Small park with mostly permanents, with clean showers and toilets. Pretty quiet at night. Middies can be an issue from around 3.30-4pm.  Park located across from the beach and about 30 mins from Cabulture or Bribie Island.

Great little caravan Park just back from the Esplanade.
Sealed roads. grassy sites. clean amenities.
Very nice place for a quiet night. Excellent amenities and lovely staff and guests. Very clean and tidy.

Best park I’ve stayed at in years . Friendly staff incredibly clean facilities and grounds old mate don’t stop except to say hi 👋 lol .good fishing quite park with friendly people too.great shop down the road get some beers too .i love the place beautiful . I wish I wasn’t leaving

Fantastic little caravan park, one of my faved in Morten bay.
As soon as you drive out of it, you see ocean.
Amenities arr kept clean and the caretakers and other guest are friendly.
It's a quiet little spot.
Excelente lugar para disfrutar en la naturaleza. Instalaciones impecables y muy cómodas. La gente amable. Vale la pena pasar por aquí
Very nice, quiet and clean caravan park.
Nice, tidy, well set up caravan park. Sites nicely spaced. Feel very close to nature

I spent a couple of weeks there. Excellent clean toilets and showers 24/7. Has a laundry with I think 3 washing machines and 2 dryers, also sinks, microwave and bar fridge, so can cook something in there or store food. All staff were excellent at customer service. I also had Kristal help me with advice to get my Ebay campervan batteries. I successfully got them fast after doing what she said. Thank you kristal 🤗. An excellent stay, thank you all!
Quiet campground.clean and well looked after

Stayed two night at the start of our holiday to be close to home in case we forgot anything, the park and amenities were well maintained, Crystal provided excellent service and good follow up (thank you) Toorbul is really quiet.

My husband and I have been to toorbul caravan park a few times it's a lovely place to stay the staff are lovely and it's a good price however my husband and I have no fixed address we are living in a tent we can only stay here for two weeks because we kill the grass we can't move all the time I have 3 mental illnesses my husband has a bad heart and back and has to be close to a hospital they have a cabin but it's 800 dollars a week why not put the price down so we can afford it and focas on him getting better he is also  my full time time carer what is more important a life or grass shame on you toorbul
Still the quietest, neatest and cleanest caravan park we've stayed at. Though I'd hate not to be able to get a site because everyone will read just how good it is here!

We stayed at this park because it was close to the Australian Zoo, Glasshouse Mountains and Bribie Island. Park was very
well presented, amenities were totally spotless, staff were great and park was very quiet.

There is a takeaway shop/pub in walking distance which we visited a few times and had great meals, locals were very friendly and chatty too.
Lovely small park, good sites ,  great price. Amenities  excellent.  Great  caretaker . Nothing much to see on town but very peaceful. Thank you

What a beautiful peaceful place too stay, we had a 2 night stay in the cottage , it was so nice and relaxing , just a short stroll to the shop and bottle shop,  office ladies very polite and helpful , Walking around the park everyone has a smile and a wave and some even stop for a chat, great place , we will be back for sure 💜
Great place to have a bit of fishing

Very clean coastal caravan park, out of the way from the hustle & bustle of the city.
Walking distance from the ocean & the corner pub.
Staff were lovely.
Will go back here again.
Awesome little caravan park, located on pumiston passage. Caretaker and office staff so friendly. Park residents make you feel so welcome . Highly recommend.

Second stay here, very nice, neat and peaceful being a boat fisher it's close to the boat ramp, pub which I would say have one of the best Parmis I have had, the park is well looked after by Ian the care taker and a good bloke to talk with and helpful.
The caretaker Ian amazing kind and careing the place looks beautiful the manager she is lovey and kind I definitely going back for an holiday again

This was a drop-dead gorgeous caravan park! The staff were truly lovely and very helpful. The amenity block was lovely. So quiet and serene. Only a few steps away from the passage between the mainland and Bribie Island.
Great staff, clean grounds and amenities. Walk to shop and water.
nice and quiet.
Great park. Quiet and good size sites. Wonderful and efficient staff.

Kleiner Campingplatz, sehr dunkel in der Nacht und daher unglaublich toller Sternenhimmel. Duschen, Campkitchen und Toiletten sehr sauber und super originell gemacht. Vor dem Campingplatz wilde Kängurus. Gerne wieder.
Not even worth 1 star go to any other campsite

Great little caravan park. Very welcoming on arrival and helpful throughout our stay. We had a powered site with easy access to all facilities. Very quiet and a number of permanents who have great pride in this place with lovely gardens around there patches and very friendly. Easy access to Bribie Island or Caboolture. Very relaxing.

I tried to extend our stay and organised with the office when I was paying and they left a note on our account. However they still booked another person into our site without letting us know.
I don't understand why they would do this knowing they would be paid by either booking on exactly the same day.
No longer quiet, full of kids.
Washing machines leak all over the floor and the dryers don't dry. You will spend $12 and three hours trying to dry one load.
If it rains expect a pool in your annexe and under your van as they do not fill all of the low spots. Do not camp here in tents in rainy weather as you will drown. We have been coming here for two years and nothing gets fixed properly. If you complain you will get booked out.
You have to pay all of your weeks upfront or they will book you out. Impossible to extend your stay no matter what unless you pay upfront.
High staff turnover.
I wonder why.
Donnybrook would be a better park.
Stayed on a powered slab site. Amenities were clean and directly behind our site. Awesome showers. Quiet area. Would stay again.

Absolutely loved my time here..peaceful and very clean . Close to everything local. No big shin dig town just local corner fish and chip shop  and bottlo rural pub. Boat ramp has fishing platoon.  Beautiful main Esplanade with picnic spots , with great views.  Clean public toilets in town. I will definitely go back. However fish and chips not the greatest at local. But other than that big thumbs up .
Big flat sites, clean amenities, friendly staff
Very good.
We will be back,  it was quiet,  clean,  and so close to the water loved it.

The caravan park is well maintained and has a nice environment.🥳
The cleanliness of the facilities depended on who was cleaning them. The guy did a fantastic job👏
The park offers free wifi but we were never able to access it at our van and the connection was 2 bars on 3G at best.👀
For some reason our air conditioner kept tripping the van and the parks power pole.
Never happened before and hasn’t happened again since very weird.😱
Terms of the stay kept changing from the booking to the arrival to the date of departure, with different office staff giving different information. All staff need to know the entire booking process. The office girl got pretty cranky wasn’t very nice to deal with.😬
If staying in the area you’ll need to stay vigilant around the mosquitos and midges,  just due to the location apart from that the area is lovely with waterfront just out the drive. Would I stay again probably not.😰

What a fantastic little park! The staff were brilliant as were the permanent residents I had the pleasure of meeting. Although we had some severe storms roll through we also had a wonderful time. Our kids enjoyed the place. The fact it’s right on the passage was the best no need to drive anywhere!
We recently stayed here in our caravan. The staff were very friendly and helpful. Very clean and well maintained park, ideally located.
Booked for one night, will be back for a longer stay .Great caravan park amenities very clean and staff friendly
Small but very good caravan park at reasonable prices. We will return.

Great hidden treasure of a Caravan Park . Very quiet , ammenities are very clean & modern , best shower pressure I’ve ever had in a Caravan Park . Lovely walk to the very friendly pub, shop & along the esplanade. Ian the park manager is a great bloke very helpful, friendly & accomodating. Peaceful & quiet if it’s a relaxing break you need this is the place to stay.
Friendly staff and very clean.
Never want to leave this beautiful, time frozen place. Don't tell anyone it's here.
Amenities clean and close to all sites. Close to the water
Nice and quiet, grounds and amenities spotless, staff very friendly 👍
Toorbul Caravan Park is a nice and relaxing place to enjoy my weekend.

Absolutely disgusted, drove 3 hrs had site booked when I got there they had changed my site without notification. New site to small no more sites available,
when you book a site number that'sthe one you expect ruined a whole weekend ,had to drive all the way home. Not worth 1 star never go back again.
Friendly staff and clean premises priced ok and central to fishing a small tavern shop..
